front: What is the fundamental definition of software quality according to ISO 25010?
back: The degree to which a software product satisfies stated and implied needs when used under specified conditions.

front: What are the two main perspectives or views of software quality defined by Garvin?
back: 1. Conformance to requirements (manufacturing view). 2. Fitness for use (user view).

front: What are the three main categories of quality characteristics defined in the ISO 25010 quality model?
back: 1. Product Quality (internal/external) 2. Quality in Use 3. Data Quality

front: What is the difference between validation and verification in the context of SQA?
back: Verification: 'Are we building the product right?' (process-focused). Validation: 'Are we building the right product?' (user needs-focused).

front: What is the purpose of a Software Quality Assurance (SQA) plan?
back: A document that defines the processes, procedures, and standards that will be used throughout the project to ensure quality. It serves as a roadmap for the SQA activities.
